evidence.summary: WebContainer 1.6.4 booted Node v22.22.3 with cross-origin isolation.
OpenClaw 2026.6.11 completed the browser lifecycle and recovered its mock session from a versioned OPFS backup in a fresh WebContainer.
The browser host retained a non-extractable AES-GCM-256 key and provider ciphertext without mounting either into WebContainer.
An OpenClaw agent completed a streamed tool round-trip through the browser-host POST https://api.openai.com/v1/responses bridge without exposing provider credentials to WebContainer.
The browser exposes a credential-and-consent-gated gpt-5.6-luna smoke test with a $0.001 displayed upper bound; automation made no live request.
A browser-owned non-extractable Ed25519 key signed the Gateway v3 challenge, completed local Control UI pairing, and reconnected with an encrypted device token.
Cold install completed in 57.1s after a 4.1% adapter improvement, warm reinstall in 2.9s, with 880 MB combined node_modules and npm-cache content.

checks.label: Exact upstream artifact
checks.detail: openclaw@2026.6.11 is pinned by version and integrity.

checks.label: Lifecycle scripts classified
checks.detail: The artifact declares lifecycle scripts; the browser probe must capture their behavior without silently skipping them.

checks.label: Published shrinkwrap consistency
checks.detail: The published manifest has 31 root declarations missing from shrinkwrap lockfile v3 and 0 mismatched declarations (31 missing dev dependencies); npm ci rejects the artifact even when dev dependencies are omitted.

checks.label: Native dependency inventory
checks.detail: 13 platform-specific package variants require capability-scoped handling.

checks.label: Browser host preflight
checks.detail: WebContainer 1.6.4 booted Node v22.22.3; built-in node:sqlite requires an adapter.

checks.label: OpenClaw WebContainer boot
checks.detail: The official artifact reached server-ready and /healthz returned HTTP 200 through explicit dependency and SQLite adapters.

checks.label: Gateway handshake
checks.detail: An authenticated WebSocket client received hello-ok for protocol 4 from OpenClaw 2026.6.11.

checks.label: Provider-independent chat turn
checks.detail: OpenClaw completed a streamed turn through a deterministic local openai-completions provider and emitted a final chat event.

checks.label: Constrained tool execution
checks.detail: The model could request only agents_list; OpenClaw executed it, returned the result to the provider, and completed the turn.

checks.label: History after reconnect
checks.detail: A new authenticated WebSocket recovered 4 messages through chat.history.

checks.label: Streaming cancellation
checks.detail: chat.abort accepted the active run and emitted an aborted event.

checks.label: Encrypted credential boundary
checks.detail: openai ciphertext persisted across document reload with a non-extractable AES-GCM-256 key; key export and wrong-scope decryption were rejected.

checks.label: Provider request policy
checks.detail: Mock transport verified an exact POST destination, store:false, redirect rejection, credential omission, secret-safe errors, and a 2 MB response limit without making a live request.

checks.label: OpenClaw host-broker turn
checks.detail: OpenClaw agent broker completed a typed-SSE streamed agents_list tool round-trip with matched Responses function_call/function_call_output input through the loopback bridge and host-owned Responses policy using gpt-5.6-luna; chat.abort reached the provider stream, credential plaintext never entered WebContainer, and no live request was made.

checks.label: User-configurable provider budget
checks.detail: The browser host enforces request, input-character, and streamed-output budgets; a custom 5-request / 120000-input / 90000-output configuration recorded 3 requests and over-limit probes failed closed.

checks.label: Protected live opt-in gate
checks.detail: A stored credential plus explicit billable-request consent unlocks one fixed-prompt gpt-5.6-luna smoke test with store:false, 128 output tokens, cancel control, completed plain-text rendering, and a $0.001 displayed upper bound; automation sent 0 live requests.

checks.label: Device pairing and token reconnect
checks.detail: A non-extractable Ed25519 key persisted in IndexedDB, completed local Control UI pairing, encrypted the issued device token, and used it for a second hello-ok while the private key remained outside WebContainer and token plaintext stayed out of diagnostics.

checks.label: OPFS runtime recovery
checks.detail: A 1010 KB mock-state backup (Clawsembly backup envelope v1, SHA-256) restored 2 transcript files in a fresh WebContainer and remained available after document reload.

checks.label: Measured browser runtime cost
checks.detail: Chromium measured 57.1s cold install (49.7s dependency repair), 2.9s warm reinstall, 619 MB node_modules, 262 MB npm cache, and 16.4s to protocol-ready. Suppressing redundant repair lifecycle scripts improved cold time by 4.1%; npm ci remains blocked by missing published shrinkwrap entries.
